title: Muzhappilangad Beach
tagline: Muzhappilangad Beach — beach town of Kerala

overview.short: Muzhappilangad Beach is a drive-in beach in Tellichery Kannur district of Kerala, on the Malabar Coast of India.
overview.description: Muzhappilangad Beach is a drive-in beach in Tellichery Kannur district of Kerala, on the Malabar Coast of India. It is 4 kilometers in length. In 2016, it was featured among the six best beaches for driving by BBC Autos. The beach is currently under major renovation.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Muzhappilangad
topPlaces.description: Muzhappilangad (മുഴപ്പിലങ്ങാട്) is a coastal census town in Kannur Municipal Corporation in Kannur district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is a suburb of Kannur city.

topPlaces.name: Anjarakandi River
topPlaces.description: The Anjarakandi River is one of two major rivers that flows through the Kannur District of Kerala in India. The river originates from the slopes of the Western Ghats bordering Wayanad and Kannur districts.

topPlaces.name: Edakkad
topPlaces.description: Edakkad is a village in Kannur city in Kannur District of Kerala state, in India.

topPlaces.name: Palayad
topPlaces.description: Palayad is a census town in Thalassery Taluk in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Dharmadam Island
topPlaces.description: Dharmadam Island (Green Island) is a small 2-hectare private island in Thalassery, Kannur District, Kerala. It lies about 100 metres from the mainland at Dharmadam.

topPlaces.name: Dharmadom
topPlaces.description: Dharmadom or Dharmadam is a Census Town in Kannur district in the state of Kerala, India. This town is located in between Anjarakandi River and Ummanchira River, and Palayad town and Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Andalurkavu
topPlaces.description: Andalurkavu is a very ancient, famous and prominent Thiyyar Community Urayima temple in Andalur in Dharmadam village of Kannur district, Kerala, India. This ancient temple is in the name of Lord Rama and the main festival is celebrated in mid-February: the first week of the month "Kumbam" of the Malayalam calendar.

topPlaces.name: Oorpazhachi Kavu
topPlaces.description: Sree Oorpazhachi Kavu is a Hindu temple in the Edakkad grama panchayat in Kannur District of Kerala state in southern India.

The Anjarakandi River is one of two major rivers that flows through the Kannur District of Kerala in India. The river originates from the slopes of the Western Ghats bordering Wayanad and Kannur districts.

Dharmadam Island (Green Island) is a small 2-hectare private island in Thalassery, Kannur District, Kerala. It lies about 100 metres from the mainland at Dharmadam.
Dharmadom or Dharmadam is a Census Town in Kannur district in the state of Kerala, India. This town is located in between Anjarakandi River and Ummanchira River, and Palayad town and Arabian Sea.

Andalurkavu is a very ancient, famous and prominent Thiyyar Community Urayima temple in Andalur in Dharmadam village of Kannur district, Kerala, India. This ancient temple is in the name of Lord Rama and the main festival is celebrated in mid-February: the first week of the month "Kumbam" of the Malayalam calendar.
Sree Oorpazhachi Kavu is a Hindu temple in the Edakkad grama panchayat in Kannur District of Kerala state in southern India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Muzhappilangad Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Muzhappilangad Beach is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Muzhappilangad Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (92 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (136 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Muzhappilangad Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Muzhappilangad Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Muzhappilangad Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Muzhappilangad Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Muzhappilangad Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Muzhappilangad, Anjarakandi River, Edakkad, Palayad, Dharmadam Island.
